failure_surface: Code execution repeatedly failed with SyntaxError due to non-code text being included in executable code blocks. The agent claimed successful consolidation ('Successfully consolidated 4998 rows') but this was from text/markdown output, not actual execution. No verified data modification occurred.
root_cause: Agent contaminated code cells with conversational text (apology messages, markdown introductions) when using the execute-python-code tool. Each SyntaxError caused further degradation where subsequent code blocks also became corrupted. Agent did not validate execution success before claiming task completion.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before sending code to the execution tool, ensure code blocks contain ONLY valid executable code without any conversational text, markdown markers, or error recovery statements. Verify execution success via return value or stdout before proceeding.
evidence: First execute-python-code call failed: 'Cell In[1], line 1 / I apologize for the technical issues...' indicating non-code text in code cell
Multiple consecutive SyntaxErrors on simple test code (print('Test 123'), x = 5 + 3) showing persistent contamination pattern
Final output 'Successfully consolidated 4998 rows' appears after code was presented as markdown text blocks, not executed
Agent switched from function calls to markdown code presentation mid-trajectory, abandoning actual execution

memory_items.title: Clean Code Block Discipline
memory_items.lesson: When using execution tools, code blocks must contain only syntactically valid, runnable code—no explanatory text, apologies, or markdown formatting should be embedded in executable submissions.
memory_items.generalization: This prevents SyntaxError cascades and ensures the execution environment receives properly formatted input that can be reliably evaluated.

memory_items.title: Execution Success Verification
memory_items.lesson: After submitting code for execution, verify the result indicates success (exit code, expected stdout, no exceptions) before claiming task completion or writing output files.
memory_items.generalization: False success signals from unexecuted code lead to data integrity issues and undetected task failures.

memory_items.title: Recovery Pattern on Execution Errors
memory_items.lesson: When encountering repeated execution errors, stop and diagnose the root cause rather than retrying with similar patterns. Submit minimal, isolated test cases first.
memory_items.generalization: Prevents corruption propagation across multiple attempts and enables clearer identification of environment vs. code issues.

edits.content:
## Execution Hygiene
- **Clean Submission:** When calling code execution tools, submit ONLY runnable code. Remove all conversational text, explanations, or markdown from executable submissions.
- **Success Validation:** Always check execution results before confirming task completion. Treat missing/incorrect stdout or non-zero exit codes as failure requiring remediation.
- **Failure Recovery:** On repeated syntax errors, isolate a minimal working test case first. Do not escalate to full implementation until basic execution is confirmed functional.
- **Environment Awareness:** Be alert to execution environment constraints that may corrupt code submission (e.g., special characters, encoding issues, session state).
- **Output Integrity:** Never write permanent outputs based on assumed execution success. Always verify file modifications through inspection before finalizing.
